notes: Windows + OpenCode + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Implement feed CRUD API and file storage
userStories.description: Support creating, updating, deleting, and querying the feed list, and write to the data/feeds index.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests are written first and cover feed create/update/delete/query.
Deleting a feed deletes its corresponding directory.
API contract documentation is consistent with tests.

userStories.title: Implement RSS fetching and article persistence
userStories.description: Fetch articles per feed and provide queries in reverse chronological order, writing to data/feeds/{feedId}/articles.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover inserting new articles and idempotency for duplicates.
Article lists are sorted by published time in descending order.
Failures for one feed do not affect fetching for other feeds.

userStories.title: Implement summary profile management
userStories.description: Support configuring multiple AI summary profiles, including name/base_url/key/model/fields/prompt_template.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover creating, updating, deleting, and querying profiles.
The uniqueness constraint for profile names is clearly enforced.
The configuration storage structure is documented.

userStories.title: Implement manual triggering of article AI summaries
userStories.description: Allow users to select an article and a profile to trigger a summary, then write the result to a markdown file.
acceptanceCriteria: Templates support variables such as title and content.
Summary results are written to `.md` files.
Tests cover mocked AI calls and writing the output to disk.

userStories.title: Implement global cascading cleanup for profile delete/edit
userStories.description: When deleting or renaming a profile, remove all summary markdown and metadata with the same name under all feed articles.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover the full scope of global cleanup.
Summaries for other profiles are not accidentally deleted.
The cleanup process tolerates missing files.

userStories.title: Implement scheduled feed fetching
userStories.description: Run background tasks to fetch feeds at fixed intervals.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover the scheduling trigger logic.
Error isolation and logging are clear.
Manual triggers and scheduled tasks can coexist.

userStories.title: Implement basic frontend page flow
userStories.description: Build the page skeletons for feed management, article list, article summary, and summary configuration, and connect them to the APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: Routing and page structure are stable.
Key interactions are covered by tests.
Empty/loading/error states are visible.
